demo_timestamps.description: General architecture development for MCP server to connect data, perform quality checks, and automate workflows.
Examples of outputs generated by MCP server, specifically listing files in an ACC project.
AI agent checking for reviews or tasks assigned in ACC
AI agent analyzing schedules and identifying inconsistencies
Synchronization of asset data between ACC and TandM

themes: Autonomous project delivery in AEC using AI and orchestration technologies

client_mentions.name: PEC
client_mentions.context: The presentation is led by Lewis Samron, detailing PEC's challenges and solutions.

ai_technologies.name: AI agents
ai_technologies.context: Used to connect data across systems and automate quality checks in project workflows

ai_technologies.name: Copilot
ai_technologies.context: Plan to transition the AI agent from Cloud to Microsoft Copilot for broader accessibility

summary: The video by Lewis Samron of PEC presents their strategy for advancing AEC project delivery from automation to autonomy using AI agents, MCP servers, GraphQL, and Autodesk APIs. It highlights the development of an AI-enabled MCP server for automated quality checks and system integration, as well as demonstrations using Autodesk products like ACC and APS to enhance workflow automation, data analysis, and asset synchronization in project management.
